page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the N1 machine family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon processor cores running at 2.3 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 26.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6.5 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, consistently rank in the bottom 25% or bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is measured at 25.86 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 21.97 GB/sec for large writes. Despite the lower raw compute speeds, the dedicated CPU allocation and 45 MB L3 cache support stable execution for workloads that require a larger memory footprint than standard general-purpose instances.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highmem-4 offers a legacy hardware profile that trades high-speed processing for a higher memory capacity per resource unit. This makes it a viable option for memory-bound applications that do not demand intensive CPU cycles. Appropriate workloads include small-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and general-purpose applications with elevated memory requirements.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the legacy N1 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz and 26.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6.5 GB per core. Benchmark results indicate modest compute capabilities, with single-core and multi-core performance generally falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth reaches 25.86 GB/sec for uncached reads. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with moderate CPU demands, such as small databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy general-purpose applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for workloads requiring moderate compute and dedicated memory resources.
